attack_surface.data_description: ICA commands sent to the remote chain, targeting privileged functions

security_gap.gap_description: ICA used to execute actions on remote chain that developer's EOA couldn't directly, by leveraging ICA's privileges.

actor_risk.description: Application Developer controls the origin EOA that directs the ICA.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If the remote ICA has excessive permissions, or can interact with vulnerable privileged contracts, the developer can abuse this to gain unauthorized control on the remote chain.

assumptions: The Interchain Account contract on the destination chain is deployed with or can be granted significant privileges (e.g., admin of other contracts).
The Application Developer can craft ICA messages to make the remote ICA call privileged functions on other contracts or perform actions it shouldn't.

mitigation.strategy: Adhere to the principle of least privilege when deploying and configuring the remote ICA contract. Carefully audit all contracts the ICA is authorized to interact with. Implement strict controls on the types of calls the ICA can make.

description: An Application Developer uses the Interchain Account system to make the remote ICA contract execute a privileged action on another contract on the destination chain, an action the developer's own EOA would not be authorized to perform directly on that chain, effectively elevating their privileges via the ICA.
